capacity_scope_description: Installation size tier for nameplate capacities of 400,000 kW (400 MW).
Installation size tier corresponding to a nameplate capacity of 1000 kW, representing a mid-scale deployment size for energy conversion technologies.
Installation size tier corresponding to a capacity of 100,000 kW (100 MW), typically representing large-scale or utility-level systems.
Nameplate capacity tier defined by a main output carrier capacity of 800,000 kW (0.8 GW), normalised per the capacity convention outlined in the nomenclature.
Installation size tier 5, representing a high-capacity scale category as defined by the original scope value.
Installation size tier corresponding to a capacity of 500 kW. Classification follows nomenclature convention for capacity normalisation (kW-based unless mass-flow specified).
Installation size tier corresponding to a specific capacity of 20,000 kg/h (20 t/h), representing a precise mass-flow rate normalization for technologies with non-electrical output carriers.
